method: Manual precision audit. For each audited pattern we drew distinct regex matches from the run 2026-06-03_v2 corpus, read each in context, and labelled it a true positive (the intended stylistic construction is genuinely present) or a false positive.
caveats: Single annotator (project author); no inter-annotator agreement, so treat as a convergent sanity check, not a gold-standard evaluation.
Precision only. Recall (patterns the regex MISSES) is not yet measured — that requires exhaustive human reading and is future work.
Audited 6 of 15 patterns and ~5–8 matches each; rare patterns lack the sample size for a stable estimate.
Counts are operationalized against each pattern's stated construct (e.g. 'power_verb_stacking' is scored as 'a power verb is present', even though the label implies clustering).

patterns.label: Not-Just-But Escalation
patterns.note: Clean. Every match is the genuine 'not just X, but Y' escalation.

patterns.label: Power Verb Stacking
patterns.note: Every match is a real power verb (optimize, accelerate, drive, revolutionize, unlock, amplify, transform, boost). BUT the regex matches single verbs, so this measures power-verb DENSITY, not the 3-in-a-row 'stacking' the label implies.

patterns.label: At-Its-Core Pretension
patterns.note: Mostly clean ('at its core', 'at the heart of', 'fundamentally changed'); one ambiguous case.

patterns.label: Tricolon (Rule of Three)
patterns.note: Fires inside longer enumerations: a 4+-item list ('Shopify, Amazon, wholesale, and ad platform') is counted as a rule-of-three. Over-counts in list-heavy copy.
false_positive_examples: Pull Shopify, Amazon, wholesale, and ad platform data (4-item list)
from your storefront, marketplaces, ads, and fulfillment systems (4-item list)

patterns.label: Em-Dash Dramatic Pivot
patterns.note: Catches em/en-dashes used as list/definition separators in bullet points, not only dramatic mid-sentence pivots.
false_positive_examples: - Quick charge — 10 minutes gives you 3 hours (spec/definition dash)
- Intuitive touch controls — adjust volume (bullet definition dash)

patterns.label: Parenthetical Aside
patterns.note: Worst performer. The regex matches ANY parenthetical; in this corpus the sample was dominated by functional annotations like '(30 characters max)' and '(24 chars)' from ad-copy prompts — none were rhetorical asides. Needs a far tighter definition or removal.
